feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Community
feed.subtitle: SAP Community blog posts in SAP Community

entry.title: Destaque da Comunidade SAP
entry.content:
É com grande alegria que recebemos este reconhecimento da Comunidade SAP para @RobertaFernande!

Seu trabalho incansável na organização dos eventos SAP pelo Brasil não apenas elevou o nível da comunidade, mas também trouxe uma visão inovadora, tornando o aprendizado mais acessível para todos os profissionais da área.

E graças também ao seu comprometimento na estruturação da página SITBrazil.com, foi possível aprimorar a organização e a distribuição geográfica dos eventos, tornando-se um marco no compartilhamento de conhecimento SAP. Esse trabalho tem criado ainda mais oportunidades para clientes, parceiros e especialistas se atualizarem sobre as mais recentes inovações e soluções.

Seu trabalho é um exemplo inspirador de transformação positiva no ecossistema SAP!

Veja a entrevista na íntegra:

Community Member Spotlight: Roberta Fernandes - Fe... - SAP Community

entry.title: Store and Use Token API Response for Authentication in SAP CPI
entry.content:
In the modern API landscape, many services require token-based authentication. These tokens often have a limited lifespan, which means they need to be refreshed periodically to maintain seamless access to APIs. SAP Cloud Platform Integration (SAP CPI) offers a streamlined way to handle such authentication mechanisms, enabling you to automate the retrieval and usage of tokens in your integration flows.

In this blog, we will walk you through the steps of retrieving a token from a Token API, storing it in a variable, and using it to authenticate subsequent API requests. By the end of this article, you’ll be equipped with the knowledge to implement a token-based authentication system in your SAP CPI iFlow.

1. Retrieve a Token from a Token API

To begin, we need to consume the Token API. This API provides the authentication token that we will store for future use. Here's how to retrieve the token:

Step 1: Use the Start Timer

Start by using the Start Timer component in your iFlow. This will allow the token API to be called at regular intervals, ensuring the token is always refreshed when required.

  • Add a Start Timer to your iFlow and configure it according to the refresh interval required by your Token API.

Step 2: Use Request-Reply to Call the API

After the Start Timer, use the Request-Reply pattern to send a request to the Token API.

  • In the Request component, select the GET method to fetch the token.
  • Map the API endpoint and any required authentication headers or parameters.

 

Step 3: Convert JSON to XML (if necessary)

If the Token API returns a response in JSON format, you will need to convert it into XML for further processing in SAP CPI.

  • Use the JSON to XML Converter to convert the response from JSON to XML.
  • If the response is already in XML format, skip this step.

 

2. Store the Token in a Variable

Once you have received the token response, it’s time to store it in a variable for easy access later in your iFlow.

Step 1: Add Write Variable

Next, add the Write Variable component to store the token.

  • In the Write Variable component, provide a suitable name for the variable (e.g., authToken).
  • Set the Type as XPath.
  • Choose the Data Type as java.lang.String.
  • In the Value field, write an XPath expression to extract the token from the API response (e.g., //Token).

Ensure that you select the Global Scope checkbox so that the variable can be accessed across different parts of your iFlow.

Step 2: End the Flow

After storing the token, connect the Write Variable component to the End component to finalize the flow.

  • Deploy your iFlow, and the token will be stored and ready for subsequent use.

 

3. Use the Token for Authenticating Subsequent API Calls

Now that the token is stored in a global variable, we can use it to authenticate subsequent API calls in the iFlow.

Step 1: Add Content Modifier

To use the stored token in your API requests, add a Content Modifier to your iFlow.

  • In the Content Modifier, add a new header.
  • Set the Header Name to the appropriate authentication header.
  • Set the Source Type to Global Variable.
  • In the Source field, specify the name of the variable where the token is stored.

This will attach the token to the header of your outgoing requests, allowing the target API to authenticate using the token.

By following these steps, you’ve automated the process of retrieving, storing, and using a token for authentication in SAP CPI. The token is periodically refreshed through the Start Timer and can be used for authenticating subsequent API calls via the Content Modifier. This ensures seamless, continuous integration without the need for manual token updates.

With this approach, you’ve built a robust authentication system for your integration flows, ensuring that your integrations remain secure and uninterrupted.

Happy Integration!

entry.title: Registrations for SAP Chota Talks Hyderabad 2025 Q1 (SAP Stammtisch Plus)
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community members,

You are cordially invited to the SAP Stammtisch Plus event happening in Hyderabad which will include an engaging &  insightful sessions on SAP various topics and networking.

Date: Saturday 22rd Feb, 2025
Time: 3:00 PM to 06:00 PM
Venue: University of Hyderabad
Prof CR Rao Road, Hyderabad Central University Rd, Gachibowli, Hyderabad, Telangana 500046
(Google Maps)

 

Please register here!! Limited seats only so hurry up (100 Participants only) 

Note: This is an In-person Event only

Why You Should Attend SAP Chota Talks (SAP Stammtish plus) 2025

@Ruthvik_Chowdary @Vasavi_Rongali @poonam_goje03 @Dileep_Saketi

entry.title: Save the Date: SAP Inside Track 2025, March 12
entry.content:
We’re excited to kick off the year with SAP Inside Track Auckland 2025, and registrations are now open! This premier event is a great opportunity for SAP professionals, enthusiasts, and thought leaders to share their insights, innovations, and expertise with a dynamic community. 

Event Overview

Date: March 12, 2025 (3:30 onwards - 6:30pm)
Location: Auckland
Venue:  Datacom Auckland, 58 Gaunt Street, Auckland Central, Auckland 1010, New Zealand

This community-driven event is all about learning, networking, and sharing insights with fellow SAP professionals, developers, and experts. Similar to the previous events, we will be running this in the evening for #SAPCommunity to catchup and network after work. We have two impactful sessions packed lined up with real-world insights and actionable strategies. These presentations will not only showcase customer success stories but also equip you with the tools and approaches to drive a smoother, more successful transformation.
 

Agenda

Welcome & SAP Community Updates (4:00pm - 4:10pm)
 
On-premise to Innovation using SAP BTP (4:10pm -4:50pm)
This presentation provides a comprehensive roadmap for modernizing legacy SAP landscapes using the power of SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP). We'll journey through a real-world case study on how we can expose SAP data as APIs for consumption in external channels with robust authentication. This session will also bring our learnings from SAUG-X and TechEd events. Finally, we'll explore the tangible business benefits unlocked through this BTP implementation, showcasing how this transformation enabled increased agility, reduced operational costs, and empowered the business with data-driven insights.
Speakers:  Mike Wallace & Jay Sandanam, Air New Zealand
 
Event Driven Architecture using SAP Advanced Event Mesh (4:50pm - 5:30pm)
Learn how real-time events and decoupled systems can drive agility, scalability, and digital transformation in your organization.
Speaker: Advit Ramesh, Accenture

Networking & Drinks (5:30pm onwards)

How to Register

We have limited spots. Register here for free and join us on March 12th. 
 
Thank you to SAP Press for sponsoring the SAP Inside Track event and a huge thanks to Datacom for offering their venue. 

 

We look forward to seeing you. Do subscribe to the Auckland Group to receive notifications of similar events.

entry.title: Why You Should Attend SAP Chota Talks Hyderabad (SAP Stammtish plus) 2025 Q1
entry.content:
In the constantly changing world of SAP technologies, being in touch with other professionals and keeping up with industry trends has never been more important. SAP CHOTA TALKS( SAP Stamtisch plus) , has become an essential platform for SAP practitioners, developers, consultants, and enthusiasts to meet, exchange ideas, and establish long-term professional contacts.

What Makes SAP Chota Talks Special?

Making the Most of Your Attendance

SAP Chota Talks 2025 is more than just another technical gathering - it's a chance to be among SAP professionals who are themselves creating the future of enterprise technology and be a part of their community. The mix of expert knowledge sharing, networking, and hands-on experiences makes it an essential investment in your professional development.
Whether you are an experienced SAP professional or just beginning, SAP Chota Talks has something for you. The relaxed atmosphere, combined with quality content and networking, provides the perfect platform for learning, sharing, and growing together.
Don't miss this special chance to boost your SAP skills, extend your professional network, and advance your career. Sign up for SAP Chota Talks 2025 now and be a part of this enriching experience.

SAP CHOTA TALKS 2025 (Q1) Schedule 

SAP CHOTA TALKS 2025

entry.title: Devtoberfest Winners Revealed
entry.content:
After weeks of coding, debugging and surviving on learning and pure determination, the results are in our Devtoberfest winners have officially been crowned!  It unfortunately did take way longer this year for the legal validation to be completed. But we are happy that the results are now final. 

Let’s take a moment to appreciate all the incredible talent last year. We are blown away by the resilient, problem-solving skills and downright genius that came out of this competition.

Whether you learned to build a groundbreaking new app or how to design the sleekest UI ever seen, you’re all winners in our hearts.

So, without further ado, let's give a massive shoutout to our incredible winners:

  1. @mclb 
  2. @iliano 
  3. @lvhengel 
  4. @Kaca1 
  5. @mdixit 
  6. @MaheshChandra 
  7. @michaelteti 
  8. @GertMertens 
  9. @vishalakshmi 
  10. @Pedro_Pereira 

But Let’s get to the exciting part…

This year’s Devtoberfest champions will receive a similar epic prize as last year: an all expenses paid trip to the main SAP TechEd in person experience -- this year in Berlin, Germany!

That’s right – you’re heading to one of the most innovative and inspiring tech gatherings on the planet. 

SAP TechEd in Berlin and SAP TechEd Virtual

November 4–6, 2025
SAP Events

Why is TechEd such a big deal?

If you haven’t heard of TechEd, you’re in for a treat. Imagine a space buzzing with top-tier developers, industry leaders and tech giants sharing latest tools, trends and ideas. It is a goldmine of learning, networking and inspirations.

 

 

We are pretty far out from the event right now and many details have yet to be determined.  But the Devtoberfest winners can already start packing their bags as their seats at the event are reserved. We will have more exciting aspects for the Devtoberfest winners as we get closer to the TechEd event dates. 

A Huge Thanks to Everyone who participated

Before we wrap up, a massive thank you to all our participants. You poured your heart and code into this, and it showed. Every project submitted was a testament to the passion and skill in our community.

So, to our winners: pack your bags, brush up on your TechEd knowledge and get ready for an unforgettable experience. And to everyone else—don’t worry, there is always next year.

Here’s to another amazing Devtoberfest! Until next time, happy coding!

Flashback to TechEd!

We've added some amazing pictures of the last year's Devtoberfest winners at TechEd 2024. Check them out for a dose of inspiration.

entry.content:

entry.title: SAP Community CWB no Business Round: Conectando Inovação e Tecnologia no Vale do Pinhão
entry.content:
Nesta segunda-feira, 17 de fevereiro, a SAP Community CWB marcou presença no Business Round, um dos eventos de networking mais importantes do ecossistema de inovação de Curitiba. Realizado no icônico Bar do Alemão, no Largo da Ordem, o evento reuniu empreendedores, investidores, gestores públicos, pesquisadores e profissionais da área de tecnologia para uma noite de conexões e oportunidades.

*A SAP Community no Palco do Business Round*

Representando a SAP Community CWB, conduzi um pitch especial, apresentando a comunidade e seus principais objetivos: fomentar o compartilhamento de conhecimento, fortalecer a troca de experiências e criar oportunidades de aprendizado e colaboração entre profissionais do ecossistema SAP.

Durante sua apresentação, destacou se os principais eventos organizados pela comunidade, como:

✅SAP Inside Track – Um evento técnico e colaborativo que reúne especialistas para compartilhar conhecimento e experiências sobre soluções SAP.

✅SAP Talk Woman – Uma iniciativa voltada para fortalecer a presença feminina no setor de tecnologia, promovendo discussões inspiradoras sobre carreira e inovação.

✅Demais eventos da SAP Community – Encontros que proporcionam networking, aprendizado e crescimento para profissionais interessados no universo SAP.

*O que torna o Business Round tão especial?*

O Business Round, organizado pela Agência Curitiba de Desenvolvimento e Inovação, tem um propósito claro: promover negócios, networking e parcerias em um ambiente descontraído e colaborativo. Nesta edição especial, os participantes puderam apresentar pitches sobre seus projetos, compartilhar oportunidades e até dar depoimentos de reconhecimento a pessoas ou iniciativas que fizeram a diferença em suas jornadas.

Para a SAP Community CWB, foi uma excelente oportunidade de ampliar sua presença no ecossistema de inovação de Curitiba e fortalecer laços com profissionais de diversas áreas. A troca de experiências e ideias reforça o compromisso da comunidade em impulsionar o crescimento e a colaboração no setor de tecnologia.

*Faça parte da SAP Community!*

Se você é apaixonado por tecnologia, inovação e quer se conectar com uma comunidade que promove aprendizado contínuo, a SAP Community é o lugar certo para você! Fique ligado nos próximos eventos e venha fazer parte dessa rede de conhecimento e oportunidades.

Acompanhe nossas redes para saber mais!

📢Juntos, impulsionamos a inovação! 🚀

#SAPCommunityCWB #Networking #Inovação #BusinessRound #ValeDoPinhão #Tecnologia

entry.title: Mentoring - Meant to Be!
entry.content:
Mentoring is a term that's commonly heard but not always fully understood. In a technical team, it's often seen as simply grooming someone on a topic, sharing knowledge, and helping them improve—these aspects are certainly part of it.

However, as you progress in your career, there comes a time when you seek advice or validation for your thoughts and approaches. At this stage, these conversations become more personal and are shared only with a select group of trusted individuals.

This is where the mentoring offered true support while grooming and preparing to take on more.

Some key takeaways from your reflection are:

  1. Collaborative Thinking: Even when someone feels competent, discussing ideas and decisions with a mentor can reveal new insights or considerations. External review can bring out nuances that may have been overlooked, ensuring a more thorough approach.

  2. Specific Feedback and Perspectives: Rather than generic advice, mentoring can provide actionable suggestions and fresh perspectives that are directly relevant to the individual’s current challenges. This can be a catalyst for improvement and helps guide the person in a clear direction.

  3. Motivation and Confidence Building: Mentoring is not just about providing technical guidance—it’s also about being a source of encouragement. Mentors often play the role of confidence boosters, reminding their mentees of their capabilities, even when self-doubt creeps in.

  4. A Trusted Support System: The most valuable aspect of mentoring, as you’ve pointed out, is having someone you can trust to validate your work. These relationships often become personal, where mentees can seek both professional advice and emotional reassurance.

  5. A Constant Source of Feedback: Having someone who can validate your work provides a level of reassurance that is crucial for personal growth. It's comforting to have a mentor who can offer constructive criticism or simply give the green light when things are on the right track.

#SLIThoughtLeadership

entry.title: Empowering New Talent: Two Innovative Projects for a Brighter Future at SAP
entry.content:
Introduction

As a new SAP employee and an immigrant who has faced the obstacles of integrating into a global company, I've spent a lot of time thinking about how we might better help and empower new colleagues. Drawing on my own experiences and a thorough examination of our current processes, I am thrilled to present three project concepts for the #ETB that I feel can make a significant difference. These initiatives: SAP Success Stories Hub, and Innovators' Lab  are my recommendations for improving the onboarding and integration experience at SAP, particularly for people who, like me, come from varied cultural backgrounds.

 

SAP Success Stories Hub: Inspiring Growth Through Shared Journeys

The Challenge:

New employees frequently lack a clear and accessible professional growth roadmap. This disparity is even more obvious for immigrant workers, who may struggle to identify role models from similar cultural or geographical origins. The lack of apparent success stories can leave many people without the motivation or assistance they need to advance their careers at SAP.

My Proposal:

I suggest establishing the SAP Success Stories Hub, a centralized digital portal that curates compelling case studies, interviews, and multimedia tales from SAP workers of all jobs, locations, and cultural backgrounds. By emphasizing varied routes and crucial milestones, this hub will allow new hires, particularly immigrants, to connect with success stories that are similar to their own experiences. Career timelines and discussion forums are interactive features that will provide realistic mentorship recommendations and encourage new employees with a clear, motivating roadmap for personal and professional advancement.

 

Innovators’ Lab: Unleashing Fresh Ideas

The Challenge:

New employees often enter SAP brimming with innovative ideas, yet the absence of a dedicated platform can result in these creative insights being overlooked. Without a structured environment for collaboration, many potential initiatives that align with our digital transformation goals remain unrealized.

My Proposal:

I propose creating an Innovators' Lab, a dedicated virtual and physical space where new employees can submit ideas, collaborate with cross-functional teams, and develop innovative solutions. Regular innovation challenges, hackathons, and brainstorming sessions, led by professional mentors and backed by leadership, will provide the resources and environment required for innovative thinking. This place will enable new personnel to put their fresh insights into actionable projects, thereby driving SAP's continuous improvement and agile innovation.

 

Conclusion

As a new hire at SAP, I’ve taken the time to reflect on how we can make the onboarding journey more enriching, especially for those coming from diverse backgrounds. The Innovators’ Lab and SAP Success Stories Hub are my humble proposals to foster collaboration, knowledge-sharing, and inspiration among employees.

That being said, SAP is a vast and innovative company, and similar initiatives may already exist. If that’s the case, I’d love to learn more about them and explore how I can contribute. I’m open to any feedback, insights, or guidance on how to get involved. So let’s connect and continue the conversation!  😊

entry.title: 🌐 Atenção, Comunidade SAP Salvador🌐
entry.content:
Temos uma notícia incrível para você! A Comunidade SAP Brasil agora tem um site oficial onde você pode acompanhar todos os eventos e ficar por dentro das últimas novidades do mundo SAP! 🚀✨

No nosso site, você encontrará:

  • Informações fornecidas sobre eventos e workshops 📅

  • Atualizações sobre as melhores práticas e tecnologias SAP 🔧

  • Notícias e artigos relevantes sobre o universo SAP 📰

  • Como participar da sua comunidade e interagir com outros membros 💬

Não perca tempo! Acesse agora o site oficial da Comunidade SAP Brasil e veja como você pode fazer parte dessa jornada incrível. Junte-se a nós e acompanhe tudo o que há de novo no mundo SAP!

🔗Acesse o site oficial e fique por dentro de tudo: 🌟 https://sitbrazil.com/

 

Vamos juntos fortalecer nossa comunidade e expandir nossos conhecimentos! 💪📈

entry.title: Seamless Integration with SAP CI: Mastering Pagination for Salesforce Adapter
entry.content:
Handling large data sets efficiently is a common challenge in integration scenarios. In SAP Cloud Integration (SAP CI), pagination plays a vital role in retrieving data in manageable chunks, ensuring seamless performance and data consistency. In this blog series, I will explore how to implement pagination across four widely-used adapters: Salesforce, Microsoft Dynamics CRM, Workday, and Coupa.

Check out the Second blog in this series on Microsoft Dynamics CRM Adapter, check it out here Seamless Integration with SAP CI: Mastering Pagination for Microsoft Dynamics CRM Adapter 

We’ll begin with Salesforce, diving into practical examples and best practices that streamline data synchronization. Whether you're dealing with customer records, transactional data, or employee information, mastering pagination will enhance your integration flows. Stay tuned as we navigate through each adapter, tackling real-world challenges and solutions.

Salesforce Adapter - Handling Large Data Sets with Custom Pagination

In this use case, we focus on fetching records from the Account entity in Salesforce, which contains 24k+ records. To achieve this, we use Salesforce Object Query Language (SOQL) to retrieve data efficiently. While SAP CI provides an Auto Pagination feature that retrieves all pages in a single call, this approach can lead to significant performance issues when dealing with large datasets.

To optimize performance and ensure seamless data retrieval, a custom pagination strategy is the recommended approach. By fetching records page by page in batch mode, we can prevent system overload, improve response times, and maintain control over data flow. In this blog, we will explore how to implement custom pagination in SAP CI for the Salesforce adapter, ensuring scalability and efficiency in your integrations.

Design Approach: 

The scenario involves sending Account entity data from Salesforce to a target system that has a batch limit of 2,000 records per request. To accommodate this constraint, we implement a custom pagination approach using two Salesforce receiver adapter calls.

The first call fetches the initial page of records, while the second call operates within a looping process to retrieve subsequent pages. Each fetched page is passed through message mapping, ensuring seamless transformation before being delivered to the target system. This cycle continues—fetching a page, processing it, and sending it to the target—until all records are transferred, ensuring an efficient full end to end Batching processing and controlled data flow from Salesforce to the target system.

Design components elaboration:

Timer : This is a timer based flow which be a scheduled as per the user's input

Content Modifier : "set properties" - This step is used to accept inputs such as EntityName, fieldnames, StartDate from the user, all these fields are externalized to make it dynamic. 

Process Call : "Initiate Salesforce Call" -  Once all the values are set, this process call is used to initiate the salesforce call

Request Reply : "Salesforce Receiver Adapter" - This step initiates the first call to salesforce to fetch the first page using SOQL query, By default, the SOQL query returns up to 2000 records on a single response page.

We are not digging into the connection tab in this blog, this blog focuses only on pagination concept.

Output:

In the response, we retrieve 2,000 records from the initial Salesforce call. The total record count is 24,446, and the response contains a <done>false</done> flag, indicating that more pages are available. Additionally, a <nextRecordsUrl> tag is provided, which serves as a reference for fetching the next batch of records.

To implement pagination, we leverage these two key fields—<done> and <nextRecordsUrl>—to construct the logic for fetching subsequent pages. As long as <done> remains false, the process continues, dynamically fetching the next set of records until all data is retrieved and processed efficiently.

 

Router : "Data?" -

A router is used to determine whether the fetched page contains any records. If records are present, the integration flow proceeds to process to next steps. However, if the page is empty, the flow terminates immediately, preventing unnecessary processing.

This check ensures optimized execution by stopping the pagination loop when there are no more records to fetch, improving efficiency and reducing unnecessary API calls.

 

Content Modifier : "Read nextURL" - This is a crucial step where are we reading two key fields—<done> and <nextRecordsUrl> using xpaths.

Process Call : "Message Mapping" - After this step, the page goes to message mapping and it is processed to the target, this step is target specific and this blog does not cover this part.

 

Router : "next batch ?" -

Identifying Next Page for Pagination

Once the first batch is successfully processed and sent to the target system, the design ensures that the integration flow moves to the next page. To achieve this, a router is used to check whether another page is available.

This check is performed using the "LoadDone" property, which was set in the previous content modifier. If more pages exist, the flow loops to fetch the next batch using the <nextRecordsUrl>. If no further pages are available, the process terminates, ensuring efficient data retrieval without unnecessary API calls.

Properties generated at the runtime for this run:

Looping Process Call : "Call next batch"

Looping Mechanism: Fetching next Pages

Once it is confirmed that more pages are available, the flow enters a looping process call to retrieve subsequent pages. This looping mechanism operates based on the condition:

${property.LoadDone} = "false"

As long as LoadDone remains "false", the process continues fetching the next batch of records using the <nextRecordsUrl>. This ensures that each page is processed and sent to the target system sequentially.

Once all pages have been retrieved and processed, LoadDone is set to "true"**, signaling that no further pages exist. At this point, the loop terminates, marking the successful completion of data transfer.

In this looping process call, there is the second request-reply step with Salesforce Adapter which will help us to fetch the next pages. 

For this call, we will make use of Operation SOQL - Execute Query for More Results and nextRecoredsURL which was set in the previous content modifier will be passed dynamically to the next "Next Records URL"

Output :

Page 2: This page has 2000 records as well, and done = false which means there are more pages.

The same Content Modifier : "Read nextURL" is used after this step to save the key properties LoadDone and nextRecordsURL.

The same Process Call : "Message Mapping" is used here to send this page to the Message Mapping and Send to Target LIP.

Final Page Handling: Completing the Pagination Process

For a total of 24,446 records, the pagination process spans 13 pages:

  • First 12 pages contain 2,000 records each.
  • Page 13 contains the remaining 446 records.

When the 13th page is fetched, the response contains:

  • done = true, indicating that this is the last batch.
  • No <nextRecordsUrl>, confirming that no more pages are available.

At this point, the looping process terminates, ensuring that all records have been successfully retrieved and processed.

Page 13 Output:

Full Run in TRACE:

 

Conclusion

Implementing custom pagination in SAP CI for the Salesforce adapter ensures efficient data retrieval while preventing performance bottlenecks. By leveraging looping mechanisms, routers, and conditional checks, we can seamlessly fetch and process large datasets in manageable batches. This approach not only optimizes API calls but also ensures smooth end-to-end data transfer. Stay tuned for the next part of this series, where we explore pagination for next adapters.

I hope this helps.

Cheers,

Punith Oswal

entry.title: SAP Forward 2025 - ASSUMTEK: Um olhar para o futuro do SAP S/4HANA
entry.content:
A comunidade SAP de Curitiba teve a oportunidade de mergulhar nas tendências mais recentes do setor durante o SAP Forward 2025, evento promovido pela Assumtek Education. Realizado na FAE Curitiba, o encontro reuniu especialistas e profissionais para debater os rumos do SAP S/4HANA, a transformação digital e as mudanças que moldarão o futuro do ERP.

Painéis inspiradores e palestrantes renomados

O evento contou com apresentações de especialistas altamente respeitados no mercado SAP, proporcionando aos participantes insights valiosos sobre desafios e oportunidades da nova geração do sistema.

🔹 Leonardo Assumpção, CEO da Assumtek, e Karin Anderle, Diretora de Operações e Recursos Humanos, deram as boas-vindas à comunidade SAP de Curitiba, destacando a importância da atualização contínua e da troca de conhecimento.

🔹 Rodrigo Andrade, profissional com mais de 24 anos de experiência no setor, trouxe uma reflexão instigante com a palestra "A Transformação Geracional no SAP - Por que o SAP Nunca Será o Mesmo?". Ele abordou as mudanças estruturais do sistema e como essas transformações impactam diretamente as estratégias de negócios.

🔹 Flávio Andrade, especialista em gestão de projetos SAP, destacou as "Competências Essenciais para Consultores SAP na Geração S/4HANA", enfatizando as habilidades mais requisitadas pelo mercado e como os profissionais podem se preparar para essa nova realidade.

🔹 Thiago Toma, referência em SAP BTP e Fiori, conduziu a sessão "BTP e Clean Core - Como se preparar para o Roadmap da SAP?", explorando a importância da Business Technology Platform (BTP) na construção de soluções escaláveis e adaptáveis às novas diretrizes tecnológicas da SAP.

🔹 Christian Geronasso trouxe um dos temas mais aguardados do evento: "AI para Consultores SAP - Como a IA Potencializa seu Trabalho". Sua apresentação mostrou como a Inteligência Artificial está revolucionando a atuação dos consultores, tornando processos mais ágeis, eficientes e estratégicos.

🔹 Leonardo Assumpção voltou ao palco para compartilhar sua visão sobre o trabalho remoto na era do S/4HANA, na palestra "Trabalhando Globalmente na Geração S/4HANA - Anywhere Office". Ele destacou como a tecnologia permite que consultores atuem de qualquer lugar do mundo, ampliando as oportunidades de carreira e colaboração.

O evento foi encerrado com um bate-papo interativo, proporcionando aos participantes a chance de esclarecer dúvidas, trocar experiências e aprofundar os temas abordados ao longo do dia.

Networking e conexões estratégicas

Além do conteúdo técnico de alto nível, o coffee break se tornou um momento estratégico para ampliar a rede de contatos. Profissionais de diversas áreas do ecossistema SAP puderam trocar experiências, compartilhar desafios e identificar novas oportunidades de colaboração.

Impacto e perspectivas para o futuro

O SAP Forward 2025 ASSUMTEK inaugurou oficialmente o calendário de eventos da Comunidade SAP de Curitiba em 2025, abrindo espaço para discussões essenciais sobre o futuro do ERP e a capacitação dos profissionais do setor. Com a adoção crescente do SAP S/4HANA e o avanço acelerado da Inteligência Artificial, iniciativas como essa se tornam ainda mais relevantes para manter os consultores e empresas alinhados com as demandas do mercado.

A expectativa é que eventos como o SAP Forward 2025 continuem impulsionando a inovação e o desenvolvimento profissional, preparando a comunidade SAP para os desafios e oportunidades

entry.title: SAP CHOTA TALKS Hyderabad 2025 (Q1) - Event Highlights
entry.content:
As the name suggests, SAP CHOTA TALKS was an amazing evening with over 75 participants. The knowledge acquired was too good to be true, a great combination of insights and deep discussions with  SAP community members.
Beginning with the lamp, lighting represents wisdom triumphing over ignorance and light chasing out darkness. This idea is encapsulated in the Sanskrit phrase "Tamaso ma jyotirgamaya".

Event started with overview on SAP Community . various SAP Events , upcoming events etc.. by@Ruthvik_Chowdary 

 

Session 1 :  Modernization Options for SAP Analytics by Koundinya Karanam .

Session emphasizes on Key Modernization Pathways, Hybrid Analytics Architecture, Integration with Contemporary Technologies, and Implementation Considerations were the main topics of this seminar.

Presentation Link :- https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/1av-eu7kd5uNstgsICIwdqU_ckExYj_Eb/edit?usp=sharing&ouid=117485857328847750250&rtpof=true&sd=true 

 Panel discussion on  "Jobs in the age of AI, distinguishing Hype from Reality" with  Dr. Mahesh Kumar CV  , Gupta Kandula  , Raghu Boddu,   & Immanuel Kolagani 

As part of the Panel below two questions covered.

Question 1: What specific skills will become more valuable as AI becomes more prevalent ?

Question 2 : How would you distinguish between jobs that AI will enhance versus those it might replace?

The first question focused more on AI-enhanced jobs and jobs at risk of replacement, particularly repetitive analysis and routine administrative tasks. Employment and Fundamental Technical Functions.
For question 2, the panel discussed their opinions and perspectives on technical literacy, human-centered skills, critical thinking and analysis, and creative and innovative thinking.

Session 2 : The Impact of AI on International Trade Presented by Dileep Kumar Saketi

Key Insights highlighted on Analysis of AI's role in reshaping global trade dynamics & emerging opportunities in international markets.

Presentation Link :- https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/1IQslQ6iKrFsbd_eQ2vtdQi5wOVVDEgEa/edit?usp=sharing&ouid=117485857328847750250&rtpof=true&sd=true

Session 3 : Integrated AI functionalities into BRIM by Swapna Borra covered on Comprehensive overview of AI integration into Business Revenue Intelligence Management (BRIM).

Link Presentation:- https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/1wiYSpd22pqRdn3zuabEzH75ZwIzdun8R/edit?usp=sharing&ouid=117485857328847750250&rtpof=true&sd=true 

The event concluded on an exciting note with a fun lucky draw session for the attending participants. Using a spinning wheel to add an element of excitement and fairness, four fortunate attendees were selected to receive commemorative t-shirts as prizes. The lucky draw brought a festive atmosphere to the closing moments of the event, rewarding participants for their engagement and attendance.

Finally event is ended with the memorable group photo.

 

 

@Ruthvik_Chowdary @Vasavi_Rongali @poonam_goje03 @Dileep_Saketi 

@KatherineKenneally @marco_v_santos @lauraschmitz

entry.title: Celebrating the Success of SAP Chota Talks – Hyderabad! 🎉
entry.content:
What an incredible day it was at SAP Chota Talks on February 22, 2025, hosted at School of Management Studies - University of Hyderabad.

This event was all about knowledge sharing, community engagement, and inspiring discussions around SAP and its evolving ecosystem.

Organizers, Volunteers & Speaker's

A huge shoutout to all the speakers, attendees, and supporters who made this event a grand success! Your enthusiasm and passion for SAP created an amazing learning environment, fostering innovation and collaboration.
From insightful sessions to thought-provoking discussions, SAP Chota Talks truly highlighted the strength of the SAP Community in India. Events like these remind us how powerful it is when like-minded professionals come together to exchange ideas and grow together.

Detailed Blog was written by @MaheshCV SAP CHOTA TALKS Hyderabad 2025 (Q1) - Event High... - SAP Community along with the presentation details 

 Let's keep the momentum going and continue to build a stronger, more connected SAP ecosystem! 🚀

entry.title: Amazing Event SAP Chota Talks -Hyderabad
entry.content:
I am had a privilege of sharing insights on Integrated scenarios of AI in #SAPBRIM as a speaker and truly inspiring to exchange knowledge with engaging and interactive audience.

SAP CHOTA TALKS Hyderabad 2025 (Q1) was propelled with SAP Business AI discussions, Panel discussions and speaker sessions.

Panel Discussion on "Jobs in Age of AI" each and every panelist  had his way of addressing the ambiguity about AI whether it enhances our jobs or over takes our Jobs.

It was great discussion hosted by Dr. Mahesh Kumar CV with  panel members Raghu Boddu, CISA, CFE, CDPSE Immanuel Kolagani Gupta Kandula

Pleasure is mine Sharing the stage with co speakers Koundinya Karanam  Dileep Kumar Saketi

Special thanks to Ruthvik Chowdary Poonam Goje Singh Dr. Mahesh Kumar CV for flawless event, your efforts are evident. 

#SAPCommunity #SITHyd

entry.title: SAP CHOTA TALKS Q1 - HYDERABAD - JUST THE BEGINNING!!
entry.content:
On February 22, 2025, the School of Management Studies at the University of Hyderabad hosted an outstanding SAP Chota Talks event. The gathering was a platform for enriching knowledge sharing, fostering community interaction, and sparking engaging discussions about the ever-changing SAP ecosystem.

The event featured insightful speaker sessions, an engaging panel discussion, and a deep dive into the impact of AI within the SAP ecosystem. These sessions provided valuable perspectives on how AI is shaping the future of SAP, sparking meaningful conversations about its potential and challenges.

It served as a wonderful platform for SAP professionals to meet, share experiences, and forge new connections. The event created a dynamic environment for networking, allowing attendees to engage with peers, exchange ideas, and build relationships that will support their growth.

@MaheshCV @Ruthvik_Chowdary Heartfelt thank you for organizing the event. Blog SAP CHOTA TALKS Hyderabad 2025 (Q1) - Event Highlights provides a thorough overview of the sessions, panel discussions, and the valuable knowledge shared during the event, offering a great resource for all those who attended and even those who couldn’t make it. We are excited and eager to see many more events like SAP Chota Talks, where knowledge is shared, connections are made, and innovation thrives.

entry.title: Getting Started with SAP CPI: A Beginner's Handbook || Part 2
entry.content:
Contents Overview

  • JSON to XML Converter
  • XML to JSON Converter
  • Request Reply
  • Send Pallet
  • Message Mapping
  • Value Mapping
  • Splitter
  • XSD File
  • OData

JSON to XML Converter:

Why we add root element?

It acts as the top-level container for all other elements defining the overall structure of the document and ensuring that there is only one parent element for every other element within the xml file.

Why we use namespace mapping?

To prevent naming conflicts between element and attributes from different sources by associating them with unique identifiers.

 

XML to JSON Converter:

Suppress Json root element: we can check this option when we want Json response without root element.

Streaming: To convert a single-line item into an array form.

Request Reply: To allows a user to call an external system and receive a response.

Send Pallet: Using send pallet for sending mail. It acts like request reply.

 

Message Mapping:

A process that transforms data between systems by mapping fields between messages.

In simple words use message mapping for converting one structure to another structure.

Why isn’t message mapping working without root?

Because it is not receiving the header node.

 

Value Mapping:

Value mapping is used to translate source values into target values during data integration.

 

Splitter:

A splitter is used to break message into smaller parts that can be processed independently.

General Splitter: Split a message into individual messages, while preserving the context of the root nodes.

Iterating Splitter: Splits a general document into its constituent elements but does not include the enclosing tags in the individual messages.

 

How to Create Service Key?

  1. Go to the instance and subscriptions.
  2. Now here we’ve to create instance.
  3. After Create the instance we’ll create service key.

* In instance we’ve to choose SAP Process Integration Process Service.

 

XSD File:

An "XSD file" stand for "XML Schema Definition" file, which essentially defines the structure and data used types of an XML message used in integration flows, allowing for data validation and ensuring the consistency of data exchanged between different systems the integration process.

An XSD file specifies the element, attributes, and their data types within an XML message, acting like a blueprint for How the data should be organized.

Validation Tool: By using an XSD, CPI can Validate incoming XML message against the defined structure, identifying any errors or in the data.

XSD files are crucial for creating message mappings in CPI. where you transform data from format (e.g. an external system) to another based on the defined structure.

OData: The OData adapter allows you to communicate with an OData protocol. you can use messages in ATOM or JSON format for Communication.

Host: This is the IP address of the server hosting the OData service.

Port: The port number on which the OData service is listening. The default ports are 80 for http and 443 for https.

Path: This is the base path for the service. it identifies the service and OData and specifies the namespace for the  entities exposed by the service.

Sublevel: A Sublevel typically refers to the depth of nested entities or related data that can be expanded in an OData query.

Entity: An "entity" represents a single, structured record of data, like a customer or product.(Set of Records)

 

Proxy type:

Internet: Internet proxy type is Suitable when your OData service is externally hosted or available on the internet. it allows the adapter to communicate with the service over the public network.

On-premise: The on-premise proxy type is suitable for Scenarios where the OData service is hosted within your organization’s network.

 

Metadata Document:

When you access the URL in your step-1, typically the service will respond with metadata about the available entity sets, properties. relationships and operations exposed by the service.

"The metadata document describes the Structure and capabilities of OData service, allowing the clients to understand how to interact with it programmatically.

In summary, when you access the full URL, you'll receive metadata and information about the available resources and operations provided by the OData service.

*.edmx is the extension of metadata.

 

EDMX (Entity Data Model XSD):

EDMX is an XML-based file that encapsulates the metadata of an OData service. it includes information about entities, properties, relationship and other aspects of the services data model.
